It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kingiella philippinensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
